title: Keyboard contract is implemented without a regression test

evidence.quote: Implements the WAI-ARIA radio button keyboard contract: one radio is in the tab order, arrow keys/Home/End move focus + selection, and Tab leaves the group.

reasoning: BucketFilter has non-trivial accessibility behavior through roving tabindex and automatic keyboard activation, but the feature metadata lists no linked tests. A regression in arrow-key, Home/End, or tab-order behavior would be easy to miss in code review because the visible click path would still work.
recommendation: Add a focused component or browser test that renders BucketFilter, verifies exactly one radio is tabbable, checks arrow/Home/End activation updates selection, and confirms clicking a selected bucket does not produce an unnecessary duplicate change.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The feature declares no tests, and the package only shows available test scripts rather than any test exercising this component.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a vitest/jsdom or Playwright-backed test for BucketFilter keyboard behavior and aria-checked/tabIndex state transitions.
minimumFixScope: A single focused test file for ui-dashboard/src/components/breach-history/bucket-filter.tsx or the owning breach-history interaction test.

history.note: Existing a11y suite already covers BucketFilter single-tab-stop, Arrow/Home/End activation, stale controlled selection roving tabindex, and axe; reran focused suite.
